comment: Literature-grounded AutoDock Vina docking scores for five NSAID analogs against COX-2 (PDB 1CX2). Until F-002's pipeline outputs ship to /assets/vina_results.json, these values stand in as ground truth for L2's ranking exercise. Numbers fall within the Vina range typically reported for these compounds against COX-2.

ligands.name: celecoxib
ligands.tagline: Selective COX-2 inhibitor. Sulfonamide head H-bonds to Arg120 and His90; trifluoromethylphenyl ring fills the Val523 side pocket.

ligands.name: rofecoxib
ligands.tagline: Methylsulfonyl variant. Slightly fewer H-bonds than celecoxib but very tight hydrophobic packing in the side pocket. Withdrawn (Vioxx) for cardiovascular reasons unrelated to docking.

ligands.name: diclofenac
ligands.tagline: Non-selective NSAID. Carboxylate H-bonds Arg120 (channel mouth), but the molecule cannot exploit the COX-2-specific side pocket — caps its affinity gain over COX-1.

ligands.name: naproxen
ligands.tagline: Naphthyl scaffold. One Arg120 anchor; methoxy group adds modest hydrophobic contact. No engagement of the Val523 pocket.

ligands.name: ibuprofen
ligands.tagline: Smallest and most flexible of the five. Single carboxylate H-bond, weak hydrophobic burial — the lowest predicted affinity in this set.

brief.title: Mission: Rank the NSAIDs
brief.biology: Five common NSAID analogs all bind COX-2, but with markedly different affinities. Two are designed for COX-2 selectivity (celecoxib, rofecoxib); three are non-selective workhorses (diclofenac, naproxen, ibuprofen). Your job is to rank them.
brief.engineering: Each pedestal lights up an analog. Trigger a pedestal to reveal that ligand's predicted ΔG (kcal/mol) on the bar chart and its narrative card. Lower (more negative) is tighter binding. Activate all five to complete the level. Compare your intuition with the Vina ground truth — the order matters more than the exact numbers.

score_hint: Bar values are AutoDock Vina ΔG (kcal/mol). More negative = tighter binding. Ranking is what matters here.
complete_message: All five ligands ranked. Notice: the COX-2 selectivity gap (celecoxib, rofecoxib) is driven by the same Val523 side-pocket geometry you exploited in Level 1, plus the sulfonamide / methylsulfonyl polar anchor.
